skill.name: notebooklm
skill.description: Use this skill to query your Google NotebookLM notebooks directly from Claude Code for source-grounded, citation-backed answers from Gemini. Browser automation, library management, persistent auth. Drastically reduced hallucinations through document-only responses.
skill.summary: Query Google NotebookLM notebooks from Claude Code for source-grounded AI answers using Gemini.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate Google NotebookLM integration using browser automation. The static findings are false positives triggered by: (1) shell command examples in documentation, (2) Playwright browser automation code for Google authentication, (3) network requests to Google's legitimate NotebookLM service. The combination of code execution + network + credentials is expected behavior for this type of integration skill.

high_findings.title: Browser Automation with Credentials
high_findings.description: Uses Playwright for browser automation with persistent authentication state. This is standard for skills that integrate with web services requiring login.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Legitimate browser automation for Google NotebookLM authentication. Uses Playwright with persistent context to maintain session cookies.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution in Scripts
medium_findings.description: Python subprocess calls for environment setup (venv creation, pip install, patchright install). These are required for skill initialization.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Subprocess calls are used for legitimate environment setup (creating venv, installing dependencies). Arguments are hardcoded strings, no user input injection.

medium_findings.title: Network Requests to Google Services
medium_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs for Google NotebookLM API endpoints. Required for integration functionality.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network requests go to notebooklm.google.com - Google's legitimate service. No data exfiltration to external servers.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access for Data Storage
low_findings.description: Accesses local filesystem to store notebook library, authentication state, and browser profile. Standard for persistent skill data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Filesystem access is for storing skill data in ~/.claude/skills/notebooklm/data/ - standard skill storage location. No sensitive system files accessed.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access
low_findings.description: Uses dotenv for optional configuration. Standard skill configuration pattern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: dotenv usage is for reading optional .env configuration file. No automatic environment variable exposure.

content.value_statement: This skill enables Claude to query Google NotebookLM notebooks directly, providing source-grounded answers from Gemini that cite your uploaded documents, drastically reducing AI hallucinations.
actual_capabilities: Query Google NotebookLM notebooks using Gemini for source-grounded answers
Manage a local library of notebooks with metadata (name, description, topics)
Handle Google authentication via browser automation with persistent sessions
Support follow-up questions with context synthesis
Store notebook data and authentication state locally in skill directory
limitations: No session persistence - each question opens a new browser session
Rate limited to 50 queries per day on free Google accounts
Manual document upload required - user must add docs to NotebookLM first
Browser automation adds latency (few seconds per question)

use_cases.title: Research Documentation Query
use_cases.description: Ask questions about your research papers, meeting notes, or study materials stored in NotebookLM and get answers backed by citations.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers, students, and knowledge workers who upload documents to NotebookLM

use_cases.title: Codebase Documentation Assistant
use_cases.description: Query your codebase documentation, READMEs, and technical specs stored in notebooks to get accurate answers about your project.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers who maintain project documentation in NotebookLM

use_cases.title: Personal Knowledge Base QA
use_cases.description: Treat NotebookLM as a personal knowledge base and ask Claude questions that retrieve specific information from your documents.
use_cases.target_user: Professionals who use NotebookLM to organize personal knowledge and notes

prompt_templates.title: Check Authentication Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if NotebookLM authentication is set up. Run: python scripts/run.py auth_manager.py status
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to verify authentication before querying notebooks

prompt_templates.title: List Available Notebooks
prompt_templates.prompt: Show all notebooks in my library. Run: python scripts/run.py notebook_manager.py list
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to see which notebooks are available for querying

prompt_templates.title: Query a Notebook
prompt_templates.prompt: Ask a question to NotebookLM: python scripts/run.py ask_question.py --question "[YOUR QUESTION]" --notebook-id [NOTEBOOK_ID]. Use the follow-up mechanism to gather complete information.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to get source-grounded answers from their notebook

prompt_templates.title: Add New Notebook
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a notebook to the library: python scripts/run.py notebook_manager.py add --url [URL] --name [NAME] --description [DESCRIPTION] --topics [TOPICS]. Use SMART ADD if content is unknown.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to register a new NotebookLM notebook for querying

output_examples.input: Ask: What is the main finding of my research notebook?
output_examples.output: Based on the documents in your notebook, the main finding is that [specific content from your documents]. This is supported by [citation from source]. The key evidence includes [additional details from documents].

output_examples.input: Ask: List all the API endpoints in my project documentation
output_examples.output: Your project documentation contains the following API endpoints: /api/users (GET, POST), /api/auth (POST), /api/documents (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE). Each endpoint is documented with request/response schemas in the OpenAPI specification section.

best_practices: Always check authentication status before attempting queries
Use the follow-up mechanism to gather complete information before responding to user
Register notebooks with accurate descriptions and topics for better searchability
Use --show-browser flag for debugging when issues occur
anti_patterns: Do not call scripts directly without the run.py wrapper
Do not skip follow-up questions - always synthesize complete answers
Do not guess notebook descriptions - use SMART ADD to discover content
Do not forget to check authentication before operations

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Google NotebookLM?
faq.answer: Run python scripts/run.py auth_manager.py setup. A browser window will open for you to manually log in to your Google account. Authentication persists for 7 days.

faq.question: Why do I need to use the run.py wrapper?
faq.answer: The run.py wrapper automatically creates and manages a virtual environment, installs dependencies, and executes scripts properly. Calling scripts directly will fail without the environment.

faq.question: How many queries can I make per day?
faq.answer: Google NotebookLM has a rate limit of 50 queries per day on free accounts. If you hit the limit, wait 24 hours or use a different Google account.

faq.question: Why does each question open a new browser?
faq.answer: This skill does not maintain persistent sessions. Each question creates a fresh browser context using stored authentication state. This is by design for reliability.

faq.question: Where is my data stored?
faq.answer: All data is stored in ~/.claude/skills/notebooklm/data/ including library.json (notebook metadata), auth_info.json (authentication status), and browser_state/ (cookies).

faq.question: How do follow-up questions work?
faq.answer: Every answer ends with 'Is that ALL you need to know?' - Claude will automatically ask follow-up questions to gather complete information before responding to you.
